name: media-downloader
description:
智能媒体下载器。根据用户描述自动搜索和下载图片、视频片段，支持视频自动剪辑。
Smart media downloader. Automatically search and download images/video clips based on user description, with auto-trimming support.
触发方式 Triggers: "下载图片", "找视频", "download media", "download images", "find video", "/media"
capabilities: Search and download images from Pexels, Unsplash, and Pixabay using natural language queries
Search and download stock videos from Pexels and Pixabay
Download videos from YouTube with yt-dlp
Trim downloaded or existing videos to specific duration using ffmpeg
Auto-trim videos to target length from the middle point
author: yizhiyanhua-ai
securitySummary: This is a legitimate media downloader skill. All 66 external command calls use hardcoded arguments for trusted tools (yt-dlp, ffmpeg). Environment variable access is for optional API keys to stock media services (Pexels, Unsplash, Pixabay). Filesystem operations are scoped to a dedicated downloads directory. No command injection or credential exfiltration patterns found.
valueStatement: Finding and downloading images and videos from stock photo services and YouTube is time-consuming and requires multiple tools. This skill automates media search and download with support for video trimming.
limitations: Requires API keys for Pexels, Unsplash, and Pixabay to search and download stock media
Requires yt-dlp and ffmpeg to be installed for YouTube and video processing features
Cannot download from platforms other than YouTube without API support
Does not support downloading playlists or channels in batch mode

useCases.title: Content Creator - Find Stock Media
useCases.description: Search for and download royalty-free images and videos for your projects without visiting multiple stock photo websites.

useCases.title: Video Editor - YouTube Clips
useCases.description: Download YouTube video clips and trim them to specific timestamps for use in your video editing workflow.

useCases.title: Developer - Media Automation
useCases.description: Automate media downloading tasks in scripts and workflows using the command-line interface.

promptTemplates.title: Basic Image Download
promptTemplates.prompt: Download 5 images of sunset from the media-downloader skill
promptTemplates.scenario: Finding stock photos for a project

promptTemplates.title: Stock Video Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Find 3 stock videos of ocean waves, maximum 30 seconds each, using media-downloader

promptTemplates.title: YouTube with Trimming
promptTemplates.prompt: Download this YouTube video https://youtube.com/watch?v=example and trim from 60 to 90 seconds using media-downloader
promptTemplates.scenario: Extracting a clip from a longer video

promptTemplates.title: Mixed Media Search
promptTemplates.prompt: Search for images and videos of mountain landscape, showing me what results are available without downloading yet

bestPractices: Set up API keys for Pexels, Unsplash, and Pixabay before use to enable stock media features
Install yt-dlp and ffmpeg system dependencies for YouTube and video trimming support
Use the --duration flag to limit video length when downloading stock videos
Use the --output flag to specify a custom download directory for your project
antiPatterns: Do not use this skill to download copyrighted content without proper licensing
Do not share your API keys in conversations or scripts that may be logged
Do not attempt to download from platforms that prohibit automated access
Do not rely on this skill for time-sensitive news content from YouTube

faq.question: Do I need API keys to use this skill?
faq.answer: API keys are optional for Pexels, Unsplash, and Pixabay. Without keys, you can still use YouTube download and video trimming features. Keys enable stock image and video search.

faq.question: What dependencies are required?
faq.answer: Install yt-dlp with pip and ffmpeg system-wide for YouTube downloads and video trimming. Run 'pip install yt-dlp' and install ffmpeg from your package manager.

faq.question: Can I download entire YouTube playlists?
faq.answer: This skill is designed for individual video downloads. For playlists, use yt-dlp directly with playlist-specific options.

faq.question: Where are files downloaded?
faq.answer: Files download to a 'downloads' folder in the skill directory by default. Use the --output or -o flag to specify a custom directory.

faq.question: What video formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill downloads MP4 by default and can trim any video format that ffmpeg supports. Use --audio-only to extract audio as MP3.

faq.question: Is the downloaded content free to use?
faq.answer: Stock media from Pexels, Unsplash, and Pixabay is royalty-free. YouTube downloads are subject to YouTube's terms. Check licensing before commercial use.
